proof_boundary: Modified signed-model target classification only; not a construction of a positive model, not an all-order recurrence theorem, not Schur positivity, not Jensen-window PF-infinity, not RH, and not Lambda <= 0.
target_problem: After the signed J-fraction, beta, Motzkin, and parity-lift diagnostics, classify which signed or modified model classes are rejected and which remain live candidates for proving [t^m]1/H(-t)>=0 for every actual zeta Jensen window.

objects.raw_motzkin_model: ordinary J-fraction Motzkin-excursion path expansion using the signed Jacobi parameters
objects.modified_model: any state-space, production-matrix, Riordan, oscillatory, or Xi/Phi cancellation construction whose final weights are manifestly nonnegative and whose coefficient extraction is exactly E(t)

required_conditions_for_live_model: Recover exactly E(t)=1/H(-t) for all degrees d and shifts n of the actual zeta heat-flow Jensen windows.
Prove coefficientwise positivity mu_m>=0 for every m, not only a finite prefix.
Use no endpoint PF, Jensen hyperbolicity, Laguerre-Polya membership, RH, or Lambda <= 0 assumption as input.
State whether the result proves only column recurrence shapes or also supplies an all-Schur/Toeplitz lift.
Survive the raw Motzkin path obstruction, beta_1 diagonal obstruction, same-length mixed-sign path obstruction, and finite-prefix promotion gates.
If the model uses cancellation, end with a manifestly nonnegative formula rather than a difference of positive quantities.

model_rows.fit: The ordinary J-fraction path expansion is the first natural way to read the signed Jacobi data as weighted Motzkin excursions.
model_rows.gap_or_rejection: It contains negative path weights already in the mu_2 cancellation row and in beta_1 diagonal rows, so the raw ordinary path sum is not a manifest positivity proof.
model_rows.required_upgrade: Replace it with a genuinely modified model whose individual final weights are nonnegative and whose total is exactly mu_m.
model_rows.proof_boundary: Rejected only as a raw manifest-positive Motzkin model; this does not reject every modified signed model.

model_rows.model_family: diagonal sign conjugation of the raw J-fraction path model
model_rows.fit: A diagonal sign conjugation is a cheap way to try to turn a signed tridiagonal or production matrix into a nonnegative one.
model_rows.gap_or_rejection: Closed path signs are invariant under diagonal sign conjugation, and the same-length mixed-sign witnesses remain mixed; the negative beta_1 diagonal obstruction also cannot be repaired this way.
model_rows.required_upgrade: Use a nontrivial state enlargement or different coefficient extraction, not just diagonal signs on the same closed paths.
model_rows.proof_boundary: Rejected as a repair of the raw closed-path model only; not a proof against oscillatory or state-space modified routes.

model_rows.fit: Multiplying all length-m paths by a global sign would be the simplest repair if every bad sign depended only on m.
model_rows.gap_or_rejection: The parity-lift obstruction has same-length closed paths H0^m and U D H0^(m-2) with opposite signs, so no global length-parity sign can make both nonnegative.
model_rows.required_upgrade: A surviving path model must alter the state space, weights, or coefficient extraction at finer resolution than path length.
model_rows.proof_boundary: Rejected as a cheap global sign repair only; it leaves state-space modified models open.

model_rows.model_family: state-space doubled or parity-resolved path model
model_rows.fit: A two-state or parity-resolved model could separate cancellations before summing and might turn the signed Jacobi data into nonnegative lifted path weights.
model_rows.gap_or_rejection: No exact lifted path model is currently constructed, a difference of two positive sums would not be enough, and the absolute-value sign-state cover of raw Motzkin paths is already rejected at mu_2 by the gap 2*kappa_1>0.
model_rows.required_upgrade: Construct a finite or countable lifted state space with nonnegative transition weights whose start-to-end path sums equal mu_m for every m,d,n, while changing more than just the signs of the raw Motzkin paths.
model_rows.proof_boundary: Live structural candidate only; the cheap absolute-value sign-lift is rejected, but no genuinely modified model or theorem is currently constructed.

model_rows.model_family: modified production matrix, Riordan array, or planar network
model_rows.fit: A nonnegative production matrix or planar network would turn reciprocal coefficients into path sums and could possibly lift beyond columns.
model_rows.gap_or_rejection: No nonnegative production matrix, Riordan representation, or network is known for the actual binomially weighted zeta windows after the raw Motzkin and sign-repair obstructions.
model_rows.required_upgrade: Produce a nonnegative production matrix generating the mu_m column sequence and state whether it proves only column positivity or all Schur/Toeplitz positivity.
model_rows.proof_boundary: Live structural candidate only; no total-positive production model is currently proved.

model_rows.fit: The signed Hankel signature, negative ordinary lambda parameters, positive kappa parameters, and beta pattern point toward an oscillatory/sign-regular matrix interpretation.
model_rows.gap_or_rejection: The oscillatory/resolvent fit matrix validates 8 theorem-fit rows with 0 ready rows, the positive-readout theorem target validates 8 candidate rows with 0 ready rows, and the positive spectral moment obstruction validates Delta_2=-g_2 plus 735 finite Delta_2<0 rows: entrywise nonnegative Jacobi powers, diagonal similarity, absolute-value majorants, classical oscillatory spectral conclusions, indefinite moment language, finite signed patterns, endpoint factorization, raw signed readouts, ordinary positive moment measures, and finite quadrature do not prove coefficientwise nonnegativity. Only a nonordinary noncircular positive spectral transform or an Xi/Phi-specific positive resolvent kernel remains live.
model_rows.required_upgrade: State and prove, or cite and verify, a theorem whose hypotheses are checked for the actual zeta windows and whose conclusion is mu_m>=0 for all m; the theorem must include an exact positive scalar readout or positive kernel, not only oscillatory spectral language.
model_rows.proof_boundary: Live theorem-search candidate only; the fit matrix sharpens the route but is not a ready-to-apply sign-regularity theorem.

model_rows.model_family: Xi/Phi-specific cancellation identity with positive final form
model_rows.fit: The direct reciprocal coefficients are positive across strong finite grids even though the raw signed path terms cancel, suggesting a possible Xi/Phi-specific positive identity.
model_rows.gap_or_rejection: No analytic identity currently rewrites mu_m as an integral, determinant, sum, or convolution with manifestly nonnegative final terms.
model_rows.required_upgrade: Derive an exact identity for the actual heat-flow coefficients whose final coefficient formula is nonnegative term-by-term or by a proved positive kernel theorem.
model_rows.proof_boundary: Live identity-search candidate only; finite positivity and cancellation pattern do not supply the identity.

model_rows.model_family: finite signed/reciprocal evidence extrapolated to an all-order theorem
model_rows.fit: The finite grids are strong falsification pressure and useful guides for theorem candidates.
model_rows.gap_or_rejection: Finite rows cannot be promoted into all degrees, all shifts, or all m by wording.
model_rows.required_upgrade: Pair the finite evidence with an independent all-order theorem or constructive model.
model_rows.proof_boundary: Finite evidence only; not a proof model.

model_rows.model_family: endpoint real-rooted or PF input for H(t)
model_rows.fit: If H(t) already has real nonpositive zeros or finite PF-infinity, then E(t)=1/H(-t) has nonnegative coefficients by a product expansion.
model_rows.gap_or_rejection: Using endpoint real-rootedness or Jensen-window PF-infinity as input assumes the target property rather than deriving it.
model_rows.required_upgrade: Use endpoint PF only as a finite consistency check or as the conclusion of a separate noncircular theorem.
model_rows.proof_boundary: Endpoint consequence only; circular as a proof input for jwpf_06.

kill_gates: Reject any proof that uses the raw ordinary Motzkin/J-fraction path expansion as manifest positivity.
Reject any proof that repairs the raw Motzkin model only by diagonal sign conjugation of closed paths.
Reject any proof that repairs the raw Motzkin model only by a global path-length parity sign.
Reject any proof that uses only an absolute-value sign-state cover of the raw Motzkin paths as a state-space doubled model.
Reject any proof that promotes finite signed-Hankel, beta, Motzkin, parity-lift, or reciprocal coefficient grids into an all-order theorem.
Reject any proof that assumes endpoint real-rootedness, Jensen-window PF-infinity, Laguerre-Polya membership, RH, or Lambda <= 0.
Reject any proof that proves only formal signed-fraction existence without coefficientwise nonnegativity of E(t).
Reject any column-only proof that claims to close jwpf_06 without an all-Schur/Toeplitz lift.

summary.main_finding: The raw ordinary Motzkin/J-fraction model, diagonal sign conjugation, global length-parity sign repair, and absolute-value sign-state cover are rejected as proof mechanisms. Four modified routes remain live only conditionally: a genuinely modified state-space doubled model, a modified production/Riordan/network model, an oscillatory sign-regular resolvent theorem narrowed by the 8-row oscillatory/resolvent fit matrix, the positive-readout theorem target, and the positive spectral moment obstruction, and an Xi/Phi-specific positive cancellation identity. Finite grids and endpoint PF language remain evidence or consequences only, not proof inputs.

invariants: Every live candidate states a missing construction, theorem, or identity.
Every rejected sign-repair row cites the Motzkin or parity-lift obstruction.
Finite evidence and endpoint PF language cannot close jwpf_06.
The artifact classifies proof routes only and does not prove coefficient positivity.
